stemHtml:
The pilot of an aircraft has received a take-off clearance:

“YR-BTO, CLIMB STRAIGHT AHEAD UNTIL 6000 FT, BEFORE TURNING RIGHT, HEADING 110. REPORT PASSING 4000 FT: RUNWAY 08L CLEARED FOR TAKE-OFF”

The flight crew is experiencing a communications receiver failure after take-off. The aircraft is below 4000 ft and the flight crew should transmit blind their next report followed by a complete repetition.

What is the correct phraseology? “YR-BTO, TRANSMITTING BLIND DUE TO RECEIVER FAILURE…

options.a: AT 4000 FT, NEXT REPORT AT 6000 FT. ALL STATIONS RADIO CHECK.”
options.b: PASSING 4000 FT, NEXT REPORT 6000 FT. CONTINUING FLIGHT AS PLANNED.”
options.c: AT 4000 FT, NEXT REPORT AT 6000 FT. HEADING 110 SPEED 160 KNOTS.”
options.d: PASSING 6000 FT, NEXT REPORT AT 1201. CONTINUING FLIGHT AS PLANNED."
